summary: UN confirmed rebel pullback from Hodeidah and two adjacent ports under truce terms.
A Houthi projectile reached Saudi territory amid an escalating cross-border salvo.
A cruise missile launched from Yemen struck a power facility inside Jizan province.
Saudi officers assumed control of Mocha and Khokha port bases vacated by Emirati troops.
Riyadh also moved soldiers into Aden and Perim Island near the Mandeb chokepoint.
Combat erupted in Aden between UAE-aligned Security Belt fighters and pro-government units.
Separatists captured the presidential complex; UN counted 40 deaths and 260 injuries.
Pro-government troops briefly entered Aden but withdrew within a day as separatists reclaimed control.
Emirati aircraft struck Yemeni government positions; Abu Dhabi cited targeting of terror cells.
Coalition air raids on a detention facility reportedly killed at least 60, per officials.
Rebel-claimed drones ignited fires at Aramco's Abqaiq plant and the Khurais oilfield.
The strikes briefly halved Saudi crude output and roiled global oil markets sharply.
Riyadh warned separatists to relinquish seized facilities or face firm consequences.
Houthi leadership pledged to suspend further attacks against Saudi territory as a de-escalation gesture.
Houthi media released video of a claimed August offensive into Najran.
Riyadh-mediated indirect negotiations between Hadi's administration and the separatists came to light.
Emirati forces transferred an airbase and Aden airport to Saudi control to ease tensions.
Both sides announced agreement on a framework for sharing power in southern provinces.
Yemen's recognised government and the STC signed the Riyadh accord in the Saudi capital.
It allocated southern ministries and was meant to return cabinet officials to Aden.
The Saudi-led coalition declared a unilateral two-week halt citing the coronavirus emergency.
After Houthi rejection of the pause, Riyadh prolonged its truce by an additional month.
The STC proclaimed autonomous southern governance, backdated to take effect at midnight.
STC fighters seized the governor's offices on Socotra island and several state buildings.
The recognised government and southern separatists agreed to halt fighting in Abyan and resume talks.
The STC withdrew its self-rule claim and committed to implementing the stalled Riyadh accord.
A CNN report documented Riyadh and Abu Dhabi handing American arms to non-state factions.
The US Central Command chief told senators he was reviewing the network's findings.
A senator wrote to the Pentagon seeking answers on arms diversion in Yemen.
A network investigation showed humanitarian food shipments being siphoned, partly by Iran-aligned rebels.
The Armed Conflict Location and Event Data Project tallied above 91000 deaths since 2015.
Hostilities flared inside Aden between Saudi-backed government forces and Emirati-trained separatist fighters.
Separatists declared themselves in command of the port city after government officials fled.
Photos confirmed separatist fighters holding eastern Aden after the government's brief reentry collapsed.
Yemen accused Abu Dhabi of striking its forces in Aden; total casualties topped 300.
Ten rebel-claimed drones struck Aramco facilities, knocking out roughly half of Saudi output.
A follow-up CNN report tracked American armoured vehicles ending up with rival southern factions.
The same senator sent a second letter after new evidence on diverted hardware.
The recognised government and southern separatists inked a Riyadh-brokered accord on Tuesday.
The senator publicly rebuked the administration after a piece on weapons reaching Aden's docks.
Rebel projectiles struck a mosque inside a Marib military camp; over eighty soldiers died.
A 24-member power-sharing cabinet brokered by Riyadh was inaugurated, splitting seats north-south.
